title: Hadrian's Inspection

intro.title: Hadrian's Inspection
intro.body:
Hadrian travelled from Rome to Britannia to inspect the northern frontier, leaving Antinous behind. The journey took him across Gaul and the Channel; he walked the line where the empire would end.

At the frontier he ordered the great wall built — eighty Roman miles of stone across the neck of the island, marking not conquest but limit. A boundary in masonry. A frontier as policy.

stops.name: Rome
stops.note: The emperor sets out from the capital on the imperial post-road, an entourage of officials and surveyors behind him, bound for the northern edge of the world.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A raeda carriage leaving a marble city gate at dawn, mounted guard alongside, cypress-lined road climbing north.

stops.name: Lugdunum
stops.note: The great road-junction of the Gauls. Fresh horses and provisions before the long haul north to the Channel shore.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A Gallo-Roman town at a river confluence, forum and aqueduct, an imperial carriage changing teams at a mansio.

stops.name: Gesoriacum
stops.note: The Channel port and base of the fleet. A galley of the Classis Britannica waits on the tide to carry the emperor across the grey water to Britannia.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A Roman harbour with a lighthouse and a low war-galley at the quay, oarsmen boarding, cold northern light on choppy sea.

stops.name: Londinium
stops.note: The province's rising town on the tidal river. Hadrian is received by the governor before turning north up the road to the legions.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A timber-and-stone provincial town on a broad river with a wooden bridge, merchant barges, a basilica rising behind.

stops.name: Eboracum
stops.note: Fortress of the Sixth Legion and the northern military capital. The emperor reviews the troops who will build and hold the wall.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A legionary fortress of stone ramparts and tiled roofs on a river bend, standards and men on parade in the courtyard.

stops.name: Vindolanda
stops.note: The frontier itself, wind and moor to the north. Here Hadrian orders the line drawn in stone across the neck of the island — a boundary as policy.
stops.imagePrompt: Classical fresco plate. A wall of dressed stone marching over bleak northern hills, a fort and gatehouse beside it, an emperor in a cloak surveying the line.

hook: The emperor walks the northern edge of the world and draws the empire's limit in stone.
